title: correa glabra var. glabra
intro: Correa glabra var. glabra, also known as the finger lime or Australian bush tomato, is a shrub native to eastern Australia with small, greenish-yellow flowers and edible, finger-shaped fruits. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of vitamin C, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Correa glabra var. glabra include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to support immune function, reduce oxidative stress, and prevent certain diseases. Correa glabra var. glabra can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or juice, and it is also used as a flavoring agent in food and beverages, particularly in its native Australia.
